Response: University of Almería (Spain); lead developer: Jose Luis Blanco-Claraco (Full Professor at University of Almería, formerly TU Munich and University of Málaga); founded 2005 by Jose Luis Blanco

Metric: Software purpose (string)
Response: Open-source cross-platform C++ library for mobile robotics research and development; provides algorithms for SLAM (Simultaneous Localization and Mapping), computer vision, motion planning, and sensor data processing; includes a 3D robotics simulation environment (RawLogViewer, SceneViewer3D); supports mapping, localization, path planning, and sensor fusion

Metric: Number of developers (all developers that have contributed at least one commit to the project) (use repo commit logs) (number)
Response: ~60 (GitHub contributors on MRPT/mrpt; primary development by Blanco-Claraco with contributions from Spanish robotics community and international collaborators)

Response: funded (Spanish national research grants; University of Almería institutional support; historical EU research project funding)

Response: 2005 (founded by Jose Luis Blanco Claraco; first public release circa 2007; continuous development since)

column4: MRPT is one of the most comprehensive single-library robotics toolkits: 100+ SLAM algorithms, 3D visualization, sensor simulation, and mobile robot-specific utilities. Includes mrpt-slam, mrpt-nav, mrpt-vision, mrpt-maps modules. Python bindings (pymrpt) available via pip install pymrpt. ROS 2 integration (mrpt_ros2 packages). Active development since 2005 with one primary maintainer (Blanco-Claraco) is remarkable.
